;<span id="Passive_review">passive review</span>
:process in which pieces of information are read passively without asking questions (as opposed to [[Glossary:Active_recall|active recall]]). For example, in '''passive review''', one might read that ''50% of marriages in the US divorce''. In [[Glossary:Active_recall|active recall]], you would have to retrieve this information from your memory: ''What proportion of marriages in the US divorce?'' [[Glossary:Active_recall|Active recall]] is far more effective in learning than '''passive review'''. In [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]], you always begin with a '''passive review''' of the learning material, and then gradually (''incrementally'') convert its portions to questions and answers suitable for [[Glossary:Active_recall|active recall]]
